mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.0 percent, labor market deterioration signals impending corporate earnings declines, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in greater than 55 percent of instances where UNRATE crosses above 5.0 percent, or signal loses predictive power in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in real retail sales and housing starts indicate broad-based consumer demand contraction, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint decline condition yields positive forward returns in greater than 60 percent of historical occurrences, or the interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Positive month-over-month growth in the index of industrial production signals supply-side recovery and rising corporate capacity utilization, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Signal produces negative forward returns in greater than 50 percent of recovery-phase observations, or out-of-sample Sharpe ratio of the signal strategy falls below 0.5.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter increases in real GDP growth reflect expanding aggregate demand and corporate revenue streams, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.01 over any 5-year rolling window, or positive GDP growth quarters are followed by negative equity returns in greater than 55 percent of cases.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 215.0, elevated inflation expectations trigger anticipated monetary tightening and margin compression, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in greater than 60 percent of months where CPIAUCSL surpasses 215.0, or the signal predictive accuracy drops below random chance in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between declining total nonfarm payrolls and rising unemployment indicates labor market slack and weakening consumer spending power, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The divergence condition fails to predict negative returns with positive returns in greater than 55 percent of cases, or the interaction effect is indistinguishable from independent proxy effects.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in housing starts signal renewed construction activity and wealth effects, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Signal yields negative forward returns in greater than 50 percent of recovery-phase observations, or the predictive relationship breaks down when housing inventory levels exceed historical averages.
